title: Expander Component Definition
description: A GPIO/Peripheral Expander component for use in Adafruit IO

setting.description: A configurable device setting. Firmware receives the selected option's 0-based index.

displayName.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI.

description.description: Optional helper text explaining what this setting controls.

default.description: Index of the default option (0-based). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

options.description: Ordered list of option labels. The selected index is sent to firmware.

expanderPin.description: A single pin exposed by a GPIO/peripheral expander.

displayName.description: The human-readable label for this pin, shown in the Adafruit IO UI.

name.description: The pin identifier passed to firmware (e.g. expander->pinMode(name, OUTPUT)).

hasGPIO.description: If true, this pin supports digital GPIO.

direction.description: For pins that have a fixed direction, the pin's direction. Leave blank if it supports both.

hasPullup.description: If true, this pin supports an internal pull-up resistor (only valid if hasGPIO is true).

hasPulldown.description: If true, this pin supports an internal pull-down resistor (only valid if hasGPIO is true).

hasPWM.description: If true, this pin supports PWM / analog output (e.g. LED constant-current drive).

hasADC.description: If true, this pin supports analog-to-digital conversion (analog input).

displayName.description: The human-friendly name of this component.

published.description: If true, this component is supported by the current firmware version and will be displayed to all users. If false, it is hidden behind a developer toggle so that contributors can still work on it against the production site.

description.description: A brief description describing this component's capabilities.

vendor.description: Name of the company that makes this component.

i2cAddresses.description: List of I2C addresses (as hex string, like "0x38") this component can appear on.

settings.description: Configurable device-level settings (e.g. gain, sample rate, integration time). Each key is the machine-readable name sent to firmware.

pins.description: List of pins exposed by this expander.
